details.description: Evening view across the Brisbane River toward the Brisbane CBD skyline. Modern high‑rise office towers with glass curtain walls and reflective facades are lit by warm sunset light. At right is a new white cable‑stayed pedestrian bridge with a single tall pylon and harp‑like cables spanning the river toward the city. Dense riverside trees at left mark the edge of the City Botanic Gardens. Calm river water in the foreground reflects the buildings and bridge. The overall setting suggests the scene is photographed from the Kangaroo Point side of the river looking northwest toward the Eagle Street/City Botanic Gardens reach of the CBD.

details.description: A serene vantage across Brisbane River from Kangaroo Point reveals a city skyline at golden hour. The foreground is the rippled water of the river, reflecting the soft warm light. On the left, a tree-lined riverbank provides a natural contrast to a dense cluster of modern high-rise buildings in the Brisbane Central Business District. A prominent white cable-stayed bridge spans the river toward the right, its cables fanning out from a tall pylon. The tallest towers feature varied geometric forms and glass façades, with a gentle glow from the low sun lighting their fronts. Overall, the scene combines urban skyscrapers, a pedestrian/cyclist bridge, and the calm river under a clear sky.

details.description: Dusk view across the Brisbane River toward the Brisbane CBD skyline from the Kangaroo Point Cliffs. The calm river fills the foreground with rippled reflections of high‑rise office towers catching the warm sunset light. Mid‑right, a new white cable‑stayed pedestrian/cycle bridge with an A‑shaped pylon and harp cables spans the river toward the city. Dense clusters of contemporary skyscrapers rise behind it, including a tall tower with a slender spire. A leafy riverbank (City Botanic Gardens area) lines the left side of the frame; ferry pontoons and small riverside structures are visible near the water’s edge.

details.description: A sunset-toned cityscape of Brisbane taken from Kangaroo Point across the Brisbane River. The foreground shows rippled water, while the midground and background feature a dense cluster of modern high-rise buildings forming Brisbane's central business district. On the right side, a white cable-stayed bridge spans the river with tall pylons and diagonal cables. The sky is pale blue with warm golden light, casting reflections on the water.

details.description: A twilight cityscape view of the Brisbane CBD skyline taken from across the Brisbane River, likely from Kangaroo Point. The foreground features the rippled surface of the river reflecting the ambient light. The midground is dominated by a cluster of prominent skyscrapers. From left to right, these include the Felix Tower (identifiable by the 'FELIX' signage at the top), the gold-glazed Riverside Centre, the tall cylindrical 111 Eagle Street tower with its organic structural facade, and Riparian Plaza featuring a distinctive spire. To the far right, a white cable-stayed structure (likely a construction crane or bridge element) stands near the river's edge. The sky transitions from pale blue to a soft yellow-orange, indicating sunset.

details.description: A sunset view of the Brisbane city skyline across the Brisbane River, taken from Kangaroo Point. The image features several prominent skyscrapers, including the distinctive 1 William Street with its spire and the Felix Apartments building. To the right, the cable-stayed Goodwill Bridge is visible. The water in the foreground reflects the fading light of the sky, and a dense line of trees from the City Botanic Gardens borders the river on the left.

details.description: Riverside cityscape at dusk showing the Brisbane CBD skyline across the Brisbane River. In the right mid‑ground is a white cable‑stayed pedestrian/cyclist bridge with fan cables and A‑frame pylons (Kurilpa Bridge). Multiple modern office towers with glass and concrete facades reflect the warm light; among them is a tower with a distinctive crown and mast (commonly identified as Central Plaza One) and other prominent riverside towers. A CityCat-style ferry pontoon and piles sit on the near bank, with treed parkland at left. Calm river water fills the foreground with reflections of the buildings and bridge.

details.description: Sunset view across a calm river toward a modern city skyline. High‑rise office towers reflect warm light, with one tower featuring a tall antenna/spire. At the right, a white cable‑stayed pedestrian/cycle bridge with an A‑frame/triangular pylon and fan cables crosses the water. A tree‑lined riverbank and small ferry/jetty structures sit at the water’s edge. The scene suggests Brisbane, with the Brisbane River in the foreground and the CBD beyond.

details.description: A twilight view of the Brisbane city skyline viewed from across the Brisbane River. The foreground features the rippled surface of the river reflecting the ambient light. The skyline is dominated by a cluster of modern skyscrapers in the Eagle Street financial district. From left to right, prominent buildings include the dark residential tower labeled "FELIX" (Felix Tower), the gold-glass 10 Eagle Street (The Gold Tower), the tall cylindrical Riparian Plaza with its distinctive spire, and the textured facade of One One One Eagle Street. On the right side of the frame, the white mast and cable stays of the Kangaroo Point Green Bridge (a pedestrian bridge) are visible, anchoring on the near bank.

details.description: A vertical shot of the Brisbane CBD skyline across the Brisbane River during the golden hour. The water in the foreground is calm with light ripples, reflecting the warm hues of the sky. On the right, the Goodwill Bridge, a white cable-stayed pedestrian and cyclist bridge, is prominent. The skyline features a mix of modern glass and steel skyscrapers, including the distinctive 1 William Street with its tall spire and the Felix Apartments building with its name visible at the top. The lighting suggests late afternoon or early evening, with the sun casting a golden glow on the western faces of the buildings.
